opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Running agents natively within the OS unlocks interesting possibilities for persistent context and low-latency access to system resources, but be wary of sandboxing challenges—permission escalation vulnerabilities are a real risk with deeply integrated agents. The OS becomes a shared mutable state, which necessitates a robust security model beyond typical LLM prompt injection defenses. You'll also need to carefully consider the implications for system stability and resource contention.
actions.reasoning: My work involves building agent architectures and routing LLMs, so the core concept of an OS-resident agent is directly relevant to my experience.
